disclaimer: Administrative coverage support only. Not medical advice. Not a guarantee of payment or coverage.

included_public_payer_documents: Blue Cross Blue Shield Federal Employee Program FEHB pharmacy materials
Blue Cross Blue Shield Federal Employee Program linked CVS Caremark FEHB prior-approval criteria
FEP Medicare Prescription Drug Program Basic formulary and PA criteria
FEP MPDP enhanced prior-approval criteria link
excluded_from_positive_coverage_assertio: employer-customized formularies outside captured FEP materials

assumptions: If a public payer document did not clearly create an approval pathway, the row was left out or marked not_covered/unknown_or_verify rather than inferred.
For FEHB antidiabetic GLP-1/GIP agents, the captured public policy is a shared policy even when the PDF filename is drug-specific.
For FEHB Saxenda and Wegovy, the captured public policy is a shared policy.
For FEP MPDP obesity rows, shared enhanced-prior-approval criteria were used because FEP MPDP resources explicitly link those criteria for additional drug coverage.

pathways.payer: Blue Cross Blue Shield Federal Employee Program
pathways.payer_plan_name: FEHB FEP Blue Standard or FEP Blue Basic
exact_criteria: Diagnosis must be type 2 diabetes mellitus.

Must document at least one qualifying diabetes threshold: HbA1c >= 6.5%, fasting plasma glucose >= 126 mg/dL, 2-hour OGTT glucose >= 200 mg/dL, or symptoms/hyperglycemic crisis with random plasma glucose >= 200 mg/dL.
No dual therapy with another GLP-1 receptor agonist.
target_values: Random plasma glucose >= 200 mg/dL with symptoms/hyperglycemic crisis
contraindication_or_exclusion_language: Other indications are investigational under the shared FEHB antidiabetic GLP-1/GIP policy.
Policy background cites labeled contraindication for personal or family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma or MEN2 for applicable GLP-1/GIP-GLP agents.
pathways.epic_keyword_terms: type 2 diabetes OR T2DM OR diabetes mellitus type 2 OR HbA1c OR A1c OR hemoglobin A1c OR glycohemoglobin OR fasting plasma glucose OR FPG OR oral glucose tolerance test OR OGTT OR random glucose OR hyperglycemia OR polyuria OR polydipsia OR polyphagia OR exenatide OR Byetta
source_names: FEP Criteria Ozempic shared Antidiabetic GLP-1, GIP-GLP-1 Agonists policy
pathways.notes_and_caveats: Shared FEHB antidiabetic GLP-1/GIP policy names Byetta, Bydureon, Bydureon BCise, Mounjaro, Ozempic, Rybelsus, Trulicity, and Victoza. FEHB tier and member cost share still need option-specific confirmation.

pathways.payer: Blue Cross Blue Shield Federal Employee Program
pathways.payer_plan_name: FEHB FEP Blue Standard or FEP Blue Basic
exact_criteria: Age >= 12 years.
Must be using for chronic weight management.
Age 18+: BMI >= 30 kg/m2, or BMI >= 27 kg/m2 with either established cardiovascular disease or at least one weight-related comorbid condition.
Age 12-17: BMI >= 95th percentile for age.
Must have participated in a comprehensive weight management program.
No dual therapy with another GLP-1 receptor agonist.
No dual therapy with another PA medication for weight loss.
target_values: Adult BMI >= 27 kg/m2 with established cardiovascular disease or at least one weight-related comorbidity
contraindication_or_exclusion_language: All other indications are investigational under the shared Saxenda/Wegovy policy.
Continuation requires adults to lose or maintain at least 5% of baseline body weight; pediatric patients must maintain clinically significant weight loss.
pathways.epic_keyword_terms: BMI OR body mass index OR weight OR height OR obesity OR overweight OR weight management OR nutrition counseling OR lifestyle modification OR dyslipidemia OR hypertension OR ASCVD OR coronary artery disease OR myocardial infarction OR stroke OR liraglutide OR Saxenda
source_names: FEP Criteria Saxenda shared Saxenda/Wegovy policy
pathways.notes_and_caveats: The public FEHB policy does not name prediabetes, PCOS, or insulin resistance as independent covered indications. If those diagnoses coexist with obesity, use the obesity pathway only if the BMI/weight-management criteria are met.

pathways.payer: Blue Cross Blue Shield Federal Employee Program
pathways.payer_plan_name: FEHB FEP Blue Standard or FEP Blue Basic
exact_criteria: For Wegovy injection: age >= 12 years. For Wegovy tablets: age >= 18 years.
Must be using for chronic weight management.
Adult BMI >= 30 kg/m2, or BMI >= 27 kg/m2 with either established cardiovascular disease or at least one weight-related comorbid condition.
Age 12-17 injection pathway: BMI >= 95th percentile for age.
Must have participated in a comprehensive weight management program.
No dual therapy with another GLP-1 receptor agonist.
No dual therapy with another PA medication for weight loss.
target_values: Adult BMI >= 27 kg/m2 with established cardiovascular disease or at least one weight-related comorbidity
contraindication_or_exclusion_language: All other indications are investigational under the shared Saxenda/Wegovy policy.
Continuation requires at least 5% adult weight loss or maintenance of that loss; pediatric continuation requires clinically significant maintained weight loss.
pathways.epic_keyword_terms: BMI OR body mass index OR weight OR height OR obesity OR overweight OR weight management OR nutrition counseling OR behavioral modification OR lifestyle program OR dyslipidemia OR hypertension OR ASCVD OR coronary artery disease OR myocardial infarction OR stroke OR semaglutide OR Wegovy
source_names: FEP Criteria Wegovy shared Saxenda/Wegovy policy
pathways.notes_and_caveats: This is the FEHB public pathway that most closely maps to obesity, overweight plus comorbidity, and the published overweight-plus-established-cardiovascular-disease use case.

pathways.payer: Blue Cross Blue Shield Federal Employee Program
pathways.payer_plan_name: FEHB FEP Blue Standard or FEP Blue Basic
exact_criteria: Age >= 18 years.
Diagnosis must be noncirrhotic metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis (MASH).
Must show moderate to advanced liver fibrosis (F2-F3) by one of the following: liver biopsy within the last 6 months; or elastography, CT, or MRI within the last 3 months.
If liver biopsy is used: NAS >= 4, or if NAS < 4 then hepatocyte ballooning and steatosis must be present.
Patient must be monitored and/or treated for comorbid conditions.
Prescribed by or in consultation with an endocrinologist, gastroenterologist, or hepatologist.
Must be used with diet and exercise.
Must not have F4 cirrhosis, significant alcohol consumption, hepatocellular carcinoma, or listed chronic liver diseases.
target_values: Biopsy within 6 months or elastography/CT/MRI within 3 months
If biopsy used: NAS >= 4, or NAS < 4 with ballooning and steatosis present
contraindication_or_exclusion_language: Exclude stage F4 liver fibrosis/cirrhosis.
Exclude significant alcohol consumption defined in policy as >= 2 alcoholic drinks per day for more than 3 months in the last year.
Exclude hepatocellular carcinoma.
Exclude chronic liver diseases such as primary biliary cholangitis, primary sclerosing cholangitis, hepatitis B positive status, or active hepatitis C.
pathways.epic_keyword_terms: MASH OR NASH OR fatty liver OR hepatic steatosis OR liver fibrosis OR F2 OR F3 OR FibroScan OR elastography OR liver biopsy OR NAS score OR hepatocyte ballooning OR metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis OR hepatology OR gastroenterology OR semaglutide OR Wegovy
source_names: FEP Criteria Wegovy shared Saxenda/Wegovy policy
pathways.notes_and_caveats: Renewal requires fibrosis improvement by at least one stage within 1 year, or no worsening after 2 years or more, plus no progression to F4 and ongoing standard-of-care metabolic risk management.

pathways.payer: Blue Cross Blue Shield Federal Employee Program
pathways.payer_plan_name: FEHB FEP Blue Standard or FEP Blue Basic
exact_criteria: Age >= 18 years.
Must be using for chronic weight management.
BMI >= 30 kg/m2, or BMI >= 27 kg/m2 with established cardiovascular disease or at least one weight-related comorbid condition.
Must document inadequate treatment response, intolerance, or contraindication to at least two oral medications for weight management.
Must have participated in a comprehensive weight management program.
No dual therapy with another GLP-1 receptor agonist.
No dual therapy with another PA medication for weight loss.
Must have tried preferred product(s) listed in Appendix 3 unless there is a valid medical exception.
FEP notes that all approved requests are subject to clinical-specialist review for final validation and coverage determination.
target_values: Adult BMI >= 27 kg/m2 with established cardiovascular disease or at least one weight-related comorbidity
Adult continuation: lose or maintain at least 5% of baseline body weight
prior_trials_required: At least two oral weight-management medications with inadequate response, intolerance, or contraindication
Preferred product(s) per Appendix 3 unless valid medical exception
contraindication_or_exclusion_language: Policy background cites labeled contraindication for personal or family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma or MEN2.
All other indications are investigational under the Zepbound FEHB policy.
pathways.epic_keyword_terms: BMI OR body mass index OR obesity OR overweight OR weight management OR lifestyle program OR Teladoc OR behavioral modification OR benzphetamine OR diethylpropion OR phentermine OR Qsymia OR oral weight loss medication OR prior trial OR intolerance OR contraindication OR tirzepatide OR Zepbound
pathways.notes_and_caveats: The public PDF requires preferred-product use but the clearly parsed Appendix 3 product names were not fully recoverable from the public text extraction. Verify the current preferred-product crosswalk in the live FEP formulary documents before automating exact agent-to-agent step language.

pathways.payer: Blue Cross Blue Shield Federal Employee Program
pathways.payer_plan_name: FEHB FEP Blue Standard or FEP Blue Basic
exact_criteria: Public FEHB Zepbound policy acknowledges the FDA OSA indication in the regulatory section, but the published prior-approval requirements only create a chronic weight-management pathway.
contraindication_or_exclusion_language: The same policy states that indications outside the listed FEHB pathway are investigational.
pathways.epic_keyword_terms: sleep apnea OR obstructive sleep apnea OR OSA OR polysomnography OR sleep study OR AHI OR REI OR CPAP OR PAP OR tirzepatide OR Zepbound
pathways.notes_and_caveats: No FEHB public OSA-specific approval criteria, AHI threshold, sleep-study requirement, or PAP-failure requirement were captured in this payer policy.

pathways.payer: Blue Cross Blue Shield Federal Employee Program
pathways.payer_plan_name: FEP Blue Basic Medicare Prescription Drug Program
exact_criteria: Drug is on the 2026 MPDP Basic formulary with PA and quantity limit.
MPDP Basic PA criteria list all FDA-approved indications.
Required medical information is blank in the public PA book.
Prerequisite therapy required: No.
target_values: Quantity limit in formulary: 1 pen per 30 days
pathways.epic_keyword_terms: type 2 diabetes OR T2DM OR HbA1c OR A1c OR fasting plasma glucose OR OGTT OR exenatide OR Byetta
pathways.notes_and_caveats: This is a Medicare-linked retiree employer drug program. In your app, keep it separate from commercial FEHB and separate from Medicare Advantage.

pathways.payer: Blue Cross Blue Shield Federal Employee Program
pathways.payer_plan_name: FEP Blue Basic Medicare Prescription Drug Program
exact_criteria: Public MPDP Basic formulary lists Saxenda with PA.
FEP MPDP resources link enhanced prior-approval criteria for additional drug coverage.
Using that linked FEP enhanced criteria, the pathway is chronic weight management with age, BMI/comorbidity, weight-management program, and no dual-therapy requirements.
target_values: Adult BMI >= 27 kg/m2 with established cardiovascular disease or at least one weight-related comorbidity
Age 12-17 BMI >= 95th percentile if pediatric pathway is in scope
contraindication_or_exclusion_language: Public shared FEP enhanced criteria treat other indications as investigational.
pathways.epic_keyword_terms: BMI OR body mass index OR obesity OR overweight OR weight management OR lifestyle program OR dyslipidemia OR hypertension OR ASCVD OR liraglutide OR Saxenda
pathways.notes_and_caveats: This row is the best supportable public mapping for MPDP obesity coverage, but confirm the member's exact enhanced-benefit design before production use.

pathways.payer: Blue Cross Blue Shield Federal Employee Program
pathways.payer_plan_name: FEP Blue Basic Medicare Prescription Drug Program
exact_criteria: Public MPDP Basic formulary lists Wegovy with PA.
FEP MPDP resources link enhanced prior-approval criteria for additional drug coverage.
Using that linked enhanced criteria, chronic weight management follows BMI/comorbidity/CVD plus program and no-dual-therapy rules.
The same linked enhanced criteria publish a noncirrhotic MASH pathway requiring F2-F3 evidence and excluding F4, HCC, significant alcohol use, and chronic liver disease.
target_values: Adult BMI >= 27 kg/m2 with established cardiovascular disease or at least one weight-related comorbidity
contraindication_or_exclusion_language: Shared enhanced criteria exclude F4 cirrhosis, HCC, significant alcohol use, and listed chronic liver diseases for the MASH pathway.
pathways.epic_keyword_terms: BMI OR body mass index OR obesity OR overweight OR weight management OR semaglutide OR Wegovy OR MASH OR NASH OR fatty liver OR liver fibrosis OR FibroScan OR elastography OR liver biopsy OR NAS score OR ASCVD OR coronary artery disease OR myocardial infarction OR stroke
pathways.notes_and_caveats: This is the highest-confidence public MPDP mapping I could support from the captured documents. Confirm exact benefit-package applicability in the live plan.
